quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd. reported a strong Q1 FY26-27 performance driven by oil prices, Jio, and Retail, despite macroeconomic challenges. The company is focusing on digital scale in Retail and maintaining operational resilience in Energy/O2C amid geopolitical volatility.

subsections.label: Consolidated performance and what drove it
points: Management framed the quarter as 'extraordinary' due to macro volatility, energy market shock, and supply chain dislocation.
Revenue increased by 25% YoY, driven by oil prices, Jio, and Retail.
EBITDA exceeded Rs. 54,000 Cr, a 10% increase.
PAT was approximately Rs. 23,200 Cr, a 6% increase.
Consumer businesses now constitute about 50% of the overall mix.
Capex was funded by cash profit, with net debt slightly lower QoQ at Rs. 1.23 lakh Cr.
Q1 capex was approximately Rs. 39,000 Cr.
Moody's upgraded RIL to Baa1 and S&P to A-.
Finance cost increased by 19% and depreciation by 9% due to Jio capitalization.

subsections.label: Oil-to-Chemicals (Refining & Marketing + Petrochem)
points: O2C revenue increased by 30% YoY; EBITDA increased by 17% to approximately Rs. 17,000 Cr.
Headline cracks/deltas overstate realizable economics due to shock costs.
RIL maintained 96-97% throughput despite logistics/availability crisis.
Middle East production fell by about 12 million bpd during this period.
RIL diversified crude sources and optimized trade/logistics.
Gas diversion to priority sectors forced more liquids, but RIL maximized gasifier throughput.
SAED drag and domestic under-recoveries were headwinds.
Planned turnaround lowered production meant for sale by 10%.
LPG production increased almost fourfold, impacting petchem feed slate.
High cracks but demand destruction outside India; India demand remained strong.

subsections.label: Petrochemicals
points: Quarter described as a roller coaster with crude changing 5-10% in a day.
Naphtha increased by 61% to $903/ton, with premiums and freight spikes.
Middle East PE supplies blocked, leading to PE delta surge.
Polyester deltas rose despite MEG delta declining.
Polymer demand was down 22%; polyester downstream hit by gas curtailment and labor disruption.
RIL's ethane advantage widened due to cheaper US associated gas.
Ethane logistics improved with new VLECs, increasing ethane availability.

subsections.label: Exploration & Production
points: Revenue increased by 3.2% YoY; EBITDA was almost flat/slightly lower.
KGD6 production and price realization were lower due to ceiling price.
CBM production crossed 1 million standard cubic meters of gas with better realizations.
Condensate realizations were high at approximately $107/bbl.
Rig arriving next month for KG basin exploration.
CBM continues 40-well program and multilaterals across both blocks.
KGD6 ceiling expected to rise to ~$9.9 in 2H.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms
points: Subscribers reached 533m; net add 35.2m in 12 months.
5G users reached 285m; home broadband 28.6m, with 14m AirFiber.
JPL revenue was Rs. 39,173 Cr, EBITDA Rs. 20,865 Cr, margin 53.3%.
Usage was 69.4 exabytes, with per-capita 43.7 GB/user/month.
ARPU was Rs. 215.6, with management denying underlying pressure.
Digital services revenue growth was ~20% YoY, outpacing connectivity.
Enterprise push is managed services-led, enabled by FWA.
78% FWA market share of net additions in India.
JPL PAT was Rs. 764 Cr despite strong EBITDA due to accounting shift.

subsections.label: Reliance Retail
points: Q1 revenue was approximately Rs. 90,000 Cr; underlying growth was 11.6%.
Customers increased by 11%; transactions by 46% vs revenue 12%.
Grocery digital orders increased by 116% YoY.
EBITDA was Rs. 6,309 Cr, slightly down YoY; margin was 7.9%.
Three-year roadmap focuses on digital commerce and hyperlocal delivery.
Quick commerce differentiation claims include transaction data and physical footprint leverage.
Grocery: 1,000+ hypermarkets; LFL +7%.
Electronics: LFL +16%; ResQ +27%.
Fashion: LFL +4%; Ajio Rush orders +136% QoQ.
RCPL revenue was Rs. 8,600 Cr, with rapid scale and supply-chain buildout.
RCPL claims #3 NARTD player in India, with plans for international expansion.

subsections.label: JioStar (Media)
points: Platform crossed 530m users; IPL reach 700m; CTV consumption +19%.
Tadka micro-content hub engaged 100m users in ~2 months.
Deep integration with OpenAI for conversational discovery.
JAMS in-house AI media studio launched AI-generated micro-content.
First in-app commerce integration with Swiggy.
Revenue was Rs. 10,946 Cr, with PBT also +14%.
Advertising hit by real-money gaming ban and US-Iran war impact.

subsections.label: Digital services and enterprise strategy
points: Digital services revenue growth was ~20% YoY, outpacing connectivity.
Enterprise push is managed services-led, bundling connectivity with security, managed Wi-Fi, ERP, analytics, and surveillance.
FWA leadership with 78% market share of net additions in India.
Patent position: only Indian tech innovator in WIPO PCT global top-20 with around 4,500 patents.

subsections.label: FWA leadership and technology/IP
points: 78% FWA market share of net additions in India.
Patent position: only Indian tech innovator in WIPO PCT global top-20 with around 4,500 patents.

subsections.label: JioStar (Media)
points: Platform crossed 530m users; IPL reach 700m; CTV consumption +19%.
Tadka micro-content hub engaged 100m users in ~2 months.
Deep integration with OpenAI for conversational discovery.
JAMS in-house AI media studio launched AI-generated micro-content.
First in-app commerce integration with Swiggy.

subsections.label: Petrochemicals
points: RIL's ethane advantage widened due to cheaper US associated gas.
Ethane logistics improved with new VLECs, increasing ethane availability.
Current ethylene feed mix: ethane and ROGC constitute about 70% of ethylene.

subsections.label: RCPL (FMCG)
points: RCPL revenue was Rs. 8,600 Cr, with rapid scale and supply-chain buildout.
RCPL claims #3 NARTD player in India, with plans for international expansion.
Manufacturing: commissioned (partially) one of the largest beverage plants in Asia.
M&A/brand integration: majority stake in Sosyo JV; operational transition completed for Toni & Guy, Brylcreem, Badedas, Matey.

subsections.label: Headwinds called out explicitly
points: SAED drag and domestic under-recoveries were headwinds.
Planned turnaround lowered production meant for sale by 10%.
LPG production increased almost fourfold, impacting petchem feed slate.
Advertising hit by real-money gaming ban and US-Iran war impact.

subsections.label: Forward view (management tone constructive but contingent)
points: Structural support for cracks from lost capacity (Middle East outages) and Russia.
Management refused near-term profitability calls due to renewed SOH volatility.
KGD6 ceiling expected to rise to ~$9.9 in 2H.

guidance_ledger.statement: KGD6 ceiling expected to rise to ~$9.9 in 2H.
Expecting to ramp up the rate of connecting new homes significantly.
On track to complete 40 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage gigafactory at Jamnagar.
Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

key_takeaways: RIL is explicitly trading near-term consumer margins for digital scale (Retail) and building new infrastructure moats (dark stores; data centers; AI/media tooling).

In Energy/O2C, management stresses realizable margin is constrained by procurement/logistics friction even when market cracks are extreme; nonetheless, RIL demonstrated operational resilience (near-full runs) and feedstock advantage (ethane-heavy slate).

Forward commentary is deliberately non-committal on near-term O2C earnings due to geopolitical volatility ('a lot of imponderables'), but management maintains confidence in structural positioning: 'structurally… we are in a good place right through.'

quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd anticipates continued growth across its segments, with a focus on expanding its digital services, retail, and new energy initiatives, despite challenges in the oil and gas sector due to geopolitical tensions.

subsections.label: Group Performance
points: The first 11 months showed robust domestic activity and significant growth in telecom traffic on the 5G network.
Rupee depreciation was a concern, with an 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Revenue was up 10% for FY2025-26, with EBITDA up 13.5%, including a one-time gain from the sale of listed shares.
Consumer businesses contributed more than 55% of EBITDA.
JPL's PAT increased by 15% year-on-year, RRVL by 12%, and RIL by 24%.
Overall growth was 13%, with digital services showing strong growth at 18%.
Retail EBITDA growth was 8%, muted by the scale-up of hyperlocal e-commerce.
O2C grew by 10% despite challenges, and Oil and Gas faced production declines.
For the quarter, EBITDA growth was flat, with consumer segments (Jio and retail) up 14%.
Net debt remained controlled, with a net debt to EBITDA ratio of 0.64.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms
points: Jio Platforms ended the year with 524 million subscribers, adding 36.3 million during the year.
The 5G user base reached 268 million, with 77 million additions during the year.
Jio Platforms revenue for the year was Rs.1,46,085 Crores, up 14.6% year-on-year.
EBITDA was Rs.76,255 Crores, with a margin of 52%, showing a 190-basis point improvement.
Total data traffic increased to 241 Exabytes for the year, a 31% increase year-on-year.

subsections.label: Retail
points: Retail business achieved the highest ever revenues of Rs.98,000 Crores for the quarter.
EBITDA for the quarter was Rs.6,900 Crores, with a margin of 7.9%.
Hyperlocal commerce showed a 30% growth in average daily orders quarter-on-quarter.
The number of registered customers grew by 11% year-on-year, with transactions up 39%.
333 new stores were opened during the quarter, crossing 20,000 stores in total.
Grocery and fashion segments showed strong double-digit growth.
JioMart serves 1200+ cities with 3100 stores, offering two-hour delivery.

subsections.label: FMCG
points: FMCG business closed with a revenue of Rs.22,000 Crores in 2026, doubling from the previous year.
Campa brand delivered Rs.4700 Crores in revenue, becoming the fourth largest carbonated soft drink brand in India.
Independence brand delivered Rs.2600 Crores in revenue, becoming a promising and trusted brand.
The package drinking water business became the third largest in India.
International presence expanded to 40 countries.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Technology Initiatives
points: Jio Platforms is implementing AI-first network optimizations, including energy optimization.
Network innovations include proprietary beam form cell design to improve coverage and capacity.
Distribution strategy involves working with OEMs to enhance user experience with new devices.
Customer experience improvements have led to Jio being rated as the best network by third-party reports.

subsections.label: JioStar Technology Initiatives
points: JioStar launched micro-content called Tadka, integrating horizontal and vertical content.
Deep product integration with OpenAI's ChatGPT transformed search functionality within the app.
In-app commerce integration with Swiggy allows users to order food during live events.

subsections.label: FMCG Manufacturing
points: FMCG is investing in manufacturing capabilities to become one of the largest manufacturers of cooldrinks in India.
The company is setting up integrated food parks to drive cost efficiencies and integrated operations.
FMCG has 12 plants across India and plans to expand further as the business grows.

subsections.label: Geopolitical and Economic Risks
points: Rupee depreciation is a concern, with an 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Supply shocks and their impact on industry and consumer confidence are major concerns.
Geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, have affected oil supply and prices.
The introduction of SAED and its impact on the business is a risk that needs to be managed.

subsections.label: Future Outlook
points: Reliance Industries anticipates continued growth across its segments, with a focus on expanding digital services, retail, and new energy initiatives.
The company is optimistic about the resilience of demand for its products, despite geopolitical challenges.

guidance_ledger.statement: Expecting to ramp up the rate of connecting new homes significantly.
On track to complete 40 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage gigafactory at Jamnagar.
Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

Impact on industry and consumer confidence.

Impact on business needs to be managed.

Jio Platforms ended the year with 524 million subscribers, adding 36.3 million during the year.
Retail business achieved the highest ever revenues of Rs.98,000 Crores for the quarter.
FMCG business closed with a revenue of Rs.22,000 Crores in 2026, doubling from the previous year.

What percentage of production is impacted by SAED?

How does the company see the quick commerce industry consolidating in the next few years?

quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd continues to see strong growth across its diversified businesses, with significant progress in new energy initiatives and robust performance in digital services and retail.

subsections.label: Group Performance
points: Revenue is up 10%, EBITDA up about 6%, and PAT at 22,290 crores, which was also up about 1.6%.
Revenue has been led by digital services and retail, with EBITDA driven by strong O2C performance, 15% higher.
Balance sheet remains steady with no change in net debt number, leverage, etc., and businesses continue to deliver cash.
S&P changed the rating from BBB Plus to A Minus, focusing on higher earnings from less cyclical businesses.

subsections.label: Retail Performance
points: Retail business had the highest ever revenue at 97,600 Crores, up 8.1%.
EBITDA came in at 6,915 Crores with a margin of 8%.
Hyperlocal commerce is scaling up rapidly with 1.6 million orders and 360% Y-O-Y growth.
Entered into an exclusive partnership with Fabletics, an American women's athleisure brand.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Performance
points: Jio Platforms ended the quarter with 515 million plus subscribers, 8.9 million net adds.
5G user base growing rapidly with 253 million plus 5G subscribers.
Revenues crossed 37,000 Crores with an EBITDA margin of 52%.
5G deployment helping win more customers with 65% share of 5G subscribers in India.

subsections.label: FMCG Performance
points: RCPL became a direct subsidiary of RIL from 1st December 2025, adding 5,000 Crores as revenue.
YTD numbers stand at 15,000 Crores.
Daily essentials grew 1.5 times Y-O-Y, and the Independence brand crossed 1,500.
Energy portfolio reached 1,000 Crores in the quarter.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Technology
points: Developed proprietary technologies across the network, with 4G and 5G running on in-house stack.
World's largest fixed wireless operator with in-house developed solutions.
Home OS and tele OS offered to homes on connected devices, scaled to over 25 million homes.
Partnerships like JioHotstar and Google Gemini Pro enhancing customer offerings.

subsections.label: New Energy Manufacturing
points: Commissioning of the first fully integrated 10-gigawatt peak annual solar manufacturing gigafactory is on track.
Solar module manufacturing has reached a high yield of 94%-95%.
Battery manufacturing is progressing with a 40-gigawatt hour capacity, expanding to 100-gigawatt hour.
Construction at Kutch for solar power generation is in full swing, with significant land development completed.

subsections.label: Oil & Gas Challenges
points: Natural decline in KG D6 fields, efforts underway to augment production.
LNG prices have moved from $11 to $9.5, impacting overall realizations.
CBM productivity improved by almost three-and-a-half to four times in recent campaigns.

subsections.label: Future Outlook
points: Strong growth expected in digital services and retail, driven by technology and innovation.
Continued focus on expanding new energy initiatives and integrated manufacturing capabilities.
Emphasis on maintaining strong cash generation and balance sheet stability.

Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

quarters.summary: Infosys anticipates a challenging FY27 with revenue growth guidance of 1.5%-3.5% CC YoY, factoring in macroeconomic headwinds and strategic decisions to not pursue certain deals. The company remains focused on AI-driven growth and strategic partnerships, while managing risks from geopolitical tensions and competitive pressures.

subsections.label: Margins
points: FY26 operating margin was 21%; Q4 was 20.9%.
Gains from currency and Project Maximus were reinvested in talent, AI, and sales and marketing.

subsections.label: Cash & Working Capital
points: DSO was 78, the lowest in 7 years.
FY26 FCF was $3.5bn (adjusted), with FCF conversion at 106% for the year and 111% for Q4.

subsections.label: Capital Return
points: Returned over $4 bn to shareholders in FY26.
Proposed final dividend of INR 25, total INR 48, which is a 11.6% increase YoY.

subsections.label: Large Deals
points: FY26 TCV for large deals was $14.9–$15.0bn, a 28% YoY increase, with 55% net new.
Q4 TCV was $3.2bn across 19 large deals.
Management emphasized that the large deal pipeline continues to remain strong.

subsections.label: Vertical/Geography Mix
points: Outperformance in Financial Services, Communications, Manufacturing, and Europe.
Communications, Manufacturing, and Europe grew more than double the company average, led by large deal wins.

subsections.label: Financial Services
points: FS grew 4.4% in FY26, driven by large-deal ramps, AI-led transformation, legacy modernization, and vendor consolidation.
Market sentiment remains positive with continued consumer spending across US banking, capital markets, and Europe.
CY26 budgets are expected to grow in the US.
Infosys is a strategic AI partner for 18 out of the top 20 clients.
Signed a large GCC deal for a regional bank in the US and a large AI-first GCC deal.

subsections.label: EURS (Energy/Utilities/Resources/Services)
points: Demand is constructive, supported by a strong large deal pipeline and vendor consolidation driven by cost takeout.
Energy sector sees increased outsourcing leading to healthy deal momentum.
Utilities face structurally higher demand due to grid constraints, renewables integration, and acceleration of electricity needs for data centers.
80% of the large deal TCV of FY26 was net new, positioned as a driver of FY27 acceleration.

subsections.label: Manufacturing
points: Cautious amid softer demand, particularly in automotive and parts of Europe.
Tariffs and ongoing Middle East conflict are causing delayed decisions.
Focus on cost optimization and operational resilience.
Near term and FY27 growth will be impacted due to low revenue from one large client.

subsections.label: Retail & Communications
points: Retail budgets are tightly controlled, with discretionary spending under pressure.
Clients want AI productivity to do more with similar budgets.
Communications budgets are flat to negative, with discretionary spending impacted.
Shift from Generative to Agentic AI, with clients consolidating IT+BPM to cut costs.
Uptick in AI deals in IT operations, software replacement, and mainframe migration.

subsections.label: AI Strategy
points: Infosys reiterated its AI thesis from Investor Day, highlighting a large addressable market for AI services across six areas: AI strategy & engineering, data, process, legacy modernization, physical AI, and trust.
Core platforms Topaz Fabric (AI) and Cobalt (cloud) are operational today.
Management pushed back on 'pilot-only' narratives, citing production-scale examples.

subsections.label: Proof Points
points: Ralph Lauren: conversational/personalized AI tool increased revenue by 12% and customer engagement by 50%.
Hertz: migrated 3 mn lines of COBOL code to microservices using foundation models, reducing cost by 60% and timeline by 60%.
BP: 50 AI agent initiatives across multiple functions, achieving 95% payment accuracy, 50% faster contract validation, and 18% improvement in IT operations efficiency.

subsections.label: Partner Ecosystem and Internal Rollout
points: New partnerships with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, NVIDIA, Microsoft, AWS, Google Cloud, and Intel.
30,000+ developers on GitHub Copilot.
90%+ of employees skilled on different AI platforms.
Fresher program includes tiered compensation for AI-aligned skills and a 'forward deployed engineer' construct for client-facing AI build work.

subsections.label: AI Revenue Disclosure
points: AI contribution is above the prior disclosed 5.5%, but management withheld updated percentages.
Management shared 5.5% at Investor Day to show materiality and growth, but are not sharing updated numbers now.

subsections.label: AI Spend Compression vs Growth
points: Management described a two-sided ledger: compression in some tech services and BPM due to efficient foundation models/tooling, and growth in AI-first work, net new large deals, and footprint expansion.
Productivity pass-through is acknowledged as a margin headwind, but growth currently exceeds compression.

subsections.label: Acquisitions/JV in Guidance
points: Stratus (insurance-related) is included in guidance, contributing ~25 bps to FY27 growth.
Optimum Healthcare and Versent JV are excluded as they are pending regulatory approvals.

subsections.label: Geopolitics
points: Management acknowledged a change in economic environment after Middle East/Iran conflict escalation, but sees paths towards stabilization.
Macro stability is tied to tech spend recovery.

subsections.label: Competitive Intensity & 'Irrational' Bids

quarters.summary: Infosys is focusing on AI-driven transformation across its services, emphasizing modernization, talent transformation, and strategic partnerships to leverage AI opportunities.

subsections.label: Tech Transitions
points: AI transition is faster than previous tech shifts, reaching 1 billion users in a couple of years compared to 10 years for the Internet and 5 years for smartphones.
AI is a fundamental change in business operations, requiring AI-native architecture and changes in customer journeys.
Modernization of legacy systems is crucial to leverage AI, as many large companies spend 60% to 80% of IT budgets on maintenance.
AI allows for faster and more economical modernization, addressing accumulated tech debt.
The balance of advantage is shifting towards 'build' rather than 'buy' due to AI's simplicity in building applications.
Foundational systems will become systems of record with agentic interfaces, simplifying user interaction.
The pace of AI change is unprecedented, with models growing from 100 billion to 1 trillion parameters in a few years.
AI technology is advancing faster than enterprise deployment capabilities, creating a deployment gap.

subsections.label: AI Services Strategy

quarters.summary: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd anticipates over 10% volume growth in FY27, supported by capacity additions and demand, while navigating geopolitical uncertainties and supply constraints.

subsections.label: Sequential margin bridge (Q4 vs Q3) — key operating levers
points: Employee cost +100 bps (benefit) due to prior quarter one-time labor code provision.
Lower discounts +50 bps.
FX tailwind +30 bps.
Fixed cost absorption +50 bps from inventory accretion.
Commodities -80 bps.
New model expenses -60 bps.
Other expenses -20 bps (CSR/R&D seasonality/lumpiness).

subsections.label: Accounting / structure note
points: Suzuki Motor Gujarat Pvt Ltd amalgamated into Maruti effective Dec 1, 2025; appointed date Apr 1, 2025; periods restated for comparability.
CFO quantified rate impact: ~46 bps blended benchmark move in the quarter drove ~INR 750 cr sequential hit (and INR 1,075 cr YoY hit).
Reversal is dynamic: “As of yesterday, some amount of reversal has already happened. Roughly about 35% of this is already reversed.”
Credit quality remains conservative: “more than 99.5% AAA-rated security.”

subsections.label: Industry context and demand shaping (FY26: two-speed year driven by GST reform)
points: Management characterized FY26 as “a year of 2 distinct halves” for Indian PVs:
H1 FY26: PV market -0.4% YoY, attributed to “affordability pressures, particularly in the small car segment.”
H2 FY26: sharp rebound +16.7% YoY, “led by the government's GST reform,” with recovery “clearly led by the small car segment, particularly vehicles in the 18% GST bracket.”
Maruti’s domestic performance showed greater cyclicality than industry:
Domestic sales -5.6% in H1, then +12.3% in H2, a “swing of about 17.9%.”
Demand was stronger than supply at year-end.

subsections.label: Key strategic/product developments (portfolio, SUVs, EVs, safety, accessibility)
points: Small car/18% GST segment regained momentum, with management noting that despite SUV-heavy market structure, “the top-selling passenger vehicle… was the Dzire” (18% GST bracket).
Mid-SUV push strengthened via VICTORIS:
“Fastest model in mid-SUV to cross 50,000 cumulative sales.”
Won Indian Car of the Year, supporting brand momentum in a high-growth segment.
EV entry: launched first BEV e VITARA:
Positioned as “first fully electric SUV developed on a fresh dedicated platform” for India and “about 100 global markets.”
“Initial response has been quite encouraging,” but management said EV volumes are currently production constrained; near-term priority is “100% customer delight” (delivery experience, home chargers).
Management also flagged EVs as a structural tailwind to ASPs: “EVs have to increase in the future. So that will help ASPs.”
Safety as a portfolio-wide commitment:
“6 airbags as standard in almost all” lineup (volumes “beyond 99%”).
ESC and ABS+EBD standard across lineup.
Bharat NCAP: 5-star for all-new Dzire, VICTORIS, e VITARA, Invicto; Baleno 4-star.
Charging ecosystem buildout:
~2,000 exclusive charging points across sales/service in 1,100 cities.
Partnered with 13 charge point operators; target is “over 1 lakh charging points across India by 2030” (with dealers/CPOs).
Inclusive mobility feature: launched WagonR with swivel seat option to aid seniors/persons with disabilities, aligned to “By Your Side” and UN SDG inequality reduction.

subsections.label: Capacity expansion and supply-side execution (material acceleration)
points: Management framed recent GST reduction as “transformative,” improving affordability and placing the sector on “sustained long-term structural growth.”
To address demand and backlog, Maruti is accelerating capacity:
Kharkhoda plant Phase 2 and Gujarat (Hansalpur) 4th line both to be operational within FY27.
Each adds 250,000 units/year, i.e., ~0.5 million units/year in one year, which management called “virtually unheard of.”
Medium-term plan: increase capacity to 4 million units per annum.
Start-up costs: CFO expects no significant drag, as “economies of scale should be able to broadly take care of the start-up cost,” assuming demand remains supportive.

key_figures.label: Export Share of India's Total PV Exports

subsections.label: Exports: scale leadership, but near-term uncertainty acknowledged
points: FY26 exports hit a record 447,000 units; Q4 exports also at record 137,215 units.
Maruti highlighted export dominance: the company “alone contributed 49% share of India's total passenger vehicle exports.”

On FY27 exports, management explicitly avoided numeric guidance due to geopolitical uncertainty: “If you can help me predict the war, I can predict the export number,” while aiming for “at least the last year numbers.”

subsections.label: Geopolitical / supply-chain risk management and cost pressure posture
points: Management cited complex operating conditions: “supply issues in rare earth metals,” West Asia conflict affecting “energy, certain raw materials and logistics.”
Mitigation actions emphasized:
Preventing production disruption (gas supply, alternate fuels, diversification).
Sourcing commodities at “lowest possible cost,” supplier coordination, contingency planning.
Leveraging strong demand and utilization (pending orders support throughput).
View on persistence: pressures acknowledged but treated as manageable—“some cost headwinds do come and it's part of business”—with expectation they could ease when the crisis abates.

subsections.label: Customer mix signals post-GST reform (evidence of entry-level revival)
points: First-time buyer mix increased materially:
Q4 buyer mix: 51% first-time, 18% replacement, 31% additional car buyers—management called it “a very clear signal” GST support is working.

subsections.label: Outlook signals (FY27): growth intent, capex, and execution priorities
points: Volume growth ambition is company-specific: management indicated expectation of “growth of over 10% this year” (Maruti), supported by capacity additions and demand.
FY27 capex: CFO guided ~INR 14,000 cr for the year.
Inventory (balance sheet) uptick explanation: mainly raw materials and WIP, plus goods in transit, positioned for “ramping up the capacity in the coming 2 quarters,” not a finished-goods build.

key_takeaways: GST reform is re-accelerating small cars: strong evidence in order book (130k small-car pending orders) and buyer mix shift (first-time buyers up to 51%).
Supply is the binding constraint, not demand, near term—low dealer stock (12 days) + large unserved orders + aggressive capacity ramp.

Margin resilience depends on execution and geopolitics: management avoided explicit margin guidance, but underscored multiple levers (discount normalization, utilization, sourcing) and expects normalization post-conflict.
Earnings volatility from treasury MTM is now a visible factor; investors should track interest-rate movements as they can swing “other income” meaningfully despite robust AAA portfolio quality.

Strategic portfolio broadening: credible mid-SUV momentum (VICTORIS) plus first BEV (e VITARA) and charging partnerships position Maruti for mix/ASP uplift, though EV volumes remain capacity constrained in the near term.

Mahindra Electric profitability: Q4 EV PBIT: ₹227 cr (₹245 cr incl. contract manufacturing).
FY26 EV: EBITDA ₹1,314 cr; PBIT +₹287 cr (incl. contract manufacturing).
Clear message: EV business is profit-positive earlier than expected.

subsections.label: Auto margins & disclosure nuance
points: Standalone Auto margin Q4: 10.9% excluding EV contract manufacturing; reported 9.5%.
FY26 Auto margin: 10.4% excl. contract mfg; reported 9.3%.
This separation is positioned as an ongoing disclosure choice to clarify ICE economics vs EV contract manufacturing effects.

subsections.label: SUV capacity roadmap (key constraint management)
points: FY25 exit capacities: ICE 54k/month, EV 5k/month.
FY26: ICE 56.5k, EV 8k (but ICE not fully realized due to engine-mix constraint).
H1 FY27 exit: ICE 60k, EV 8k.
FY27 end: add +10k ICE (for NU_IQ-related products launching FY28) and +4k EV (new EVs for FY28).

subsections.label: Nagpur plant
points: Targeted start mid-2028; land acquisition nearing completion; 2-year execution plan.

subsections.label: Supply chain headwinds (near-term) and mitigation
points: Gas: 'very difficult but has not caused any disruption'; improved in last 2–3 weeks.
DRAM / memory chips: treated as a structural constraint, requiring inventory build and aftermarket purchases despite inflationary impact.

subsections.label: Exports
points: M&M became 5th largest exporter, 'only 37 units away from being number 4,' positioning this as evidence of international scaling optionality.

subsections.label: Growth Gems: operational turnarounds + scale-up vectors
points: Growth Gems profit +50% YoY (Q4 PAT +94%).
Aero structures: 'close to a billion dollars of orders in a little over a year'; ambition: Top-5 global aero structures.
Logistics: management explicitly reversed prior stance: 'not very good execution… That has changed now.'
Advanced Technologies (defence): momentum tied to higher armed forces spend.
Trucks & buses / SML: acquisition integrated well; 'gained market share marginally,' better positioned for growth.
Real Estate: profit ₹298 cr; management emphasized sustainability.
Powerol / Accelo: profit callouts (₹276 cr and ₹224 cr, respectively); Accelo noted at ~50% share in organized auto recycling with regulatory tailwinds referenced.

subsections.label: What management is explicitly worried about (and how they’re positioning)
points: Persistent uncertainty: CEO’s macro view: shocks accelerating in frequency; strategy is 'Accelerate in Uncertainty', not defensiveness.
Inflation/commodities: acknowledged recent inflation; pricing approach is calibrated.
Supply chain: DRAM/memory chips treated as long-duration structural tightness; gas stabilized.
Tractor seasonality + rainfall: downside risk skewed to H2 if rainfall deficit manifests later in the year.

subsections.label: FY27 operational outlook, capacity roadmap, and product cadence
points: Tractors: ~5% (mid-single digit).
SUVs (M&M growth, not industry): mid-to-high teens.
<3.5T LCV: high single digits.

subsections.label: FY27 launch comment
points: 'All new in the F27? There's nothing all new. But… more than capacity ramp up, so wait and watch.'

guidance_ledger.statement: Tractor growth mid-single digit (~5%).
SUVs (M&M growth, not industry): mid-to-high teens.
<3.5T LCV: high single digits.

Revenue increased by 22% at the consolidated level.
EPS for the quarter is 36.4, showing significant growth from the previous year.

quarters.summary: Bajaj Auto achieved record performance in Q1 FY27 despite operational disruptions, with strong growth in exports and electric vehicles. The company plans to expand capacity and focus on premium and electric segments.

subsections.label: Cost & Margin Management
points: CFO called Q1 'hyperinflationary' with commodity inflation rising to ~4.5% of revenue.
Specific increases cited: steel +10%, aluminum/platinum +~40%, rhodium +~40%, plus ABS, copper, nickel, natural rubber.
Inflation broadened into electronics, proprietary components, energy, logistics, labor.
Pricing offset 'nearly about half' of inflation with pricing in Apr & Jun.
Additional offsets: rupee depreciation and discretionary cost rationalization.
Despite cost shock, EBITDA margin 20.9% (+10 bps QoQ, +110 bps YoY).

subsections.label: Capital Allocation, Balance Sheet, and Subsidiaries
points: Standalone FCF ~₹2,300cr (≈80% of PAT); surplus cash ₹21,000cr at quarter-end.
Shareholder returns: CFO clarified July outflows include dividend + recently concluded buyback.
Total payout aligned to prior commitment of 100% payout of FY26 profit (~₹9,825cr).
Cash expected to dip post-July and rebuild toward ~₹15,000cr by FY-end.
Consolidation: Q1 consolidated includes first full quarter of Bajaj Mobility AG, reducing YoY comparability.
Consolidated revenue ₹21,689cr (+65% YoY); consolidated PAT ₹3,226cr (+46% YoY).
Bajaj Auto Credit (BACL/BACM): income ₹1,100cr+, PAT ₹227cr (>2x YoY); AUM ~₹20,000cr (+70% YoY); CAR 19%; ROE >25% (as of Jun 2026).

subsections.label: Forward View
points: Export run-rate ambition: move beyond '250,000 per month' from Q2 onward (vs ~200k/month only a few quarters earlier).
Domestic 2W: portfolio 'turnaround program' and heavy launch calendar.

key_figures.label: EV and Export Contribution to Domestic Revenue

subsections.label: Segment Mix Shift
points: Management highlighted exports and EV as standout: 'Taken together, our electric two-wheeler and three-wheeler businesses delivered their largest ever quarter and are now at 30% of domestic revenues with double digit EBITDA percentage.'
EV profitability update (explicit): EV portfolio (2W+3W) remains 'double digit' EBITDA margin; Chetak moved from 'EBITDA neutral' to 'EBITDA positive.'
Exports: record volumes, broad-based share gains; aiming >250k/month.

subsections.label: Premium Exports
points: KTM exports from India recovered: +20% YoY; Triumph brand exports +40% YoY.

subsections.label: Domestic CV (3W incl. e‑rickshaws)
points: Industry (incl. e‑rickshaws) grew ~11% YoY; e‑autos doubled, now 44% of L5.
Bajaj delivered highest ever billings and retail; ICE share ~70%; EV leadership maintained.
L3/e‑rickshaw: new product 'RICI' in ~150 cities; company frames L3 as upgrade opportunity via quality/reliability/ownership experience.
Noted: L3 segment highly disorganized and largely lead-acid; regulatory pressure is limiting lead-acid permits; this is expected to drive lead-acid → Li-ion migration and upgrading.

subsections.label: Near-term Launch Blitz
points: Within ~6 weeks: complete 'exhaustive portfolio makeover' with:
'an absolutely new 150cc under the Pulsar brand'
'10 facelifts in the 160 to 400cc range' with 'class-leading features,' more electronics, and improved engine performance
'an absolutely new 125cc' Pulsar + upgrades

Later in FY27: introduce 'two new brands in the 125cc segment' (new nameplates, not Pulsar), with differentiated propositions; strategic aim includes upgrading 100cc customers into 125cc where innovation headroom and profitability are better.

subsections.label: Capacity Management
points: Capacity is now the binding constraint; plan to expand ~25% from 7mn to ~9mn units/year focused on EV 2W/3W, high-end motorcycles, and 3Ws.
Demand exceeds supply; capacity: currently ~50k/month, immediate unlock to ~60k/month via productivity/vendor actions; broader capacity expansion underway.

subsections.label: Africa
points: Industry grew ~50%; Bajaj grew ~2x that; retail 'doubled,' led by Boxer 125 Heavy Duty (upgrade).
Nigeria: pre-election institutional demand; Bajaj claims it captured disproportionate growth with ~60% retail market share; driven by Boxer equity and 'wide footprint of retail and service network.'

subsections.label: Latin America
points: Continued strong outperformance, especially Mexico (noted as 'amongst the top five markets of the world').
Drivers: success of NS / N250 models, 'well-organized supply chain in Mexico' and favorable tariffs (management cited 3%), plus distribution expansion (75 stores).
Region also positioned as a driver of 'corporate capital' (i.e., profitability/cash).

subsections.label: Asia
points: Muted due to Bangladesh/Nepal; focus shifted to defending share and dealer health.
Philippines: multiple launches + exclusive motorcycle stores to build the premium/personal motorcycling category.

subsections.label: Supply Disruptions Quantified
points: Multiple concurrent shocks: industrial fuel supply curtailment, West Asia logistics force majeure impacting alloys/polymers, labor shortages due to cost-of-living + elections.
Estimated lost production opportunity: ~10% of volume.

subsections.label: Industry Context (Demand Shape)
points: Domestic 2W registrations +14% YoY (Q1), slower than Q4 (+25%); growth led by EV scooters and 150–400cc motorcycles.
100cc & 125cc segments were 'flattish,' resulting in ~7% industry motorcycle growth.

Management interpretation: stress is concentrated 'down the pyramid'; higher-income cohorts remain stronger. They explicitly linked sentiment drag to West Asia crisis and household fuel pressures ('LPG availability into every kitchen').

subsections.label: Bajaj Strategy: De-emphasize 100cc on Profitability Grounds
points: Clear positioning: in 100cc, Bajaj will participate 'at our own terms,' and where 'growth and profitability… get into a conflict… we tend to favor profitability and are prepared to lose market share.'
Rationale: 100cc structurally shrinking (management cited industry mix decline from ~55% to mid‑40s over 5–7 years) and low 'degrees of freedom' for innovation.

subsections.label: Outlook and Strategic Priorities (FY27)
points: Management expects supply-chain disruptions to ease in Q2; rupee weakness seen as a buffer, but inflation/logistics remain key risks.
Explicit focus areas listed for FY27:
Domestic motorcycles: win in 125cc+ via portfolio makeover and new brands.
Exports: sustain share gains; push beyond 250k/month.
Premium (KTM/Triumph): accelerate via network expansion and niche category build (adventure/sport).
Electric: capture rapid growth in 2W & 3W; improve availability.
Capacity management to maximize availability.
Support KTMAG turnaround to 'normative level.'
Continue scaling and performance in BACL.

guidance_ledger.statement: Move beyond 250,000 per month from Q2 onward (vs ~200k/month only a few quarters earlier).
Win in 125cc+ via portfolio makeover and new brands.
Accelerate via network expansion and niche category build (adventure/sport).
Capture rapid growth in 2W & 3W; improve availability.
Expand ~25% from 7mn to ~9mn units/year focused on EV 2W/3W, high-end motorcycles, and 3Ws.
The 150cc plus segment should grow at twice the industry rate.
We expect to outpace industry growth in the 125cc plus segment and particularly in the 150cc plus segment and continue on the path of market share acquisition.
We expect to start to scale up Riki beginning quarter 1.
The industry should improve its growth rate by 6 to 8 percentage points.
We are taking steps to build three-wheeler capacity, particularly in the e-vehicle segment.
We will aim to maintain the leadership position here on.

risk_register.note: Management expects disruptions to ease in Q2, but inflation/logistics remain key risks.
Commodity inflation rose to ~4.5% of revenue, with specific increases in steel, aluminum, platinum, and rhodium.
West Asia logistics force majeure impacting alloys/polymers.
Noble metals, including rhodium, platinum and palladium are further heating up.
Inflation could diminish the purchasing power of customers, impacting growth.
Constraints in e-components and manufacturing capacity impacted EV segment.
Cost pressures across key commodities, including steel and noble metals.

themes.label: Export growth
themes.note: Exports hit a new high with broad-based share gains and record volumes.

themes.label: Electric vehicle growth
themes.note: EVs delivered their largest ever quarter, now at 30% of domestic revenues.

themes.label: Commodity cost pressures
themes.note: Commodity inflation rose to ~4.5% of revenue, impacting margins.

themes.label: Geopolitical risks
themes.note: West Asia logistics force majeure impacting alloys/polymers.

themes.label: Capacity expansion
themes.note: Plan to expand capacity by ~25% to meet demand, especially in EVs.

key_takeaways: Management described Q1 as “the highest quarterly performance across all parameters” with 1.4mn units, ₹17,244cr revenue, ₹3,596cr EBITDA, ~₹3,000cr PAT and 20.9% EBITDA margin.
The quarter was operationally volatile: RM inflation, supply chain/logistics disruptions, and a ransomware attack.
Combined disruptions impaired availability by ~10–15%, “more so in exports, high-end bikes, and EVs.”

Management highlighted exports and EV as standout: “Taken together, our electric two-wheeler and three-wheeler businesses delivered their largest ever quarter and are now at 30% of domestic revenues with double digit EBITDA percentage.”
Exports BU hit a new high of 732k units and ~USD 735m revenue (as stated), contributing ~40% of Bajaj Auto revenue.
Domestic 2W: portfolio “turnaround program” and heavy launch calendar.
Capacity is now the binding constraint; plan to expand ~25% from 7mn to ~9mn units/year focused on EV 2W/3W, high-end motorcycles, and 3Ws.
Management expects supply-chain disruptions to ease in Q2; rupee weakness seen as a buffer, but inflation/logistics remain key risks.

quarters.summary: Bajaj Auto anticipates continued growth in exports and electric vehicles, despite challenges in the domestic market and rising commodity costs.

subsections.label: Quarterly Performance
points: Revenue at its highest crossed INR16,000 crores.
EBITDA came in at INR3,323 crores with margins at 20.8%.
Volumes grew 24%, translating into a 32% increase in revenues.
Profit after Tax for the quarter stood at INR2,746 crores, registering 34% year-on-year growth.

subsections.label: Annual Performance
points: FY '26 closed with the highest ever annual volumes for the company at over 5 million units.
Revenue followed through at nearly INR59,000 crores, up 17% year-on-year.
EBITDA closed the year at a new high of INR12,000 upwards crores, growing 19% year-on-year with margins at 20.5%.
Profit after tax came in at INR9,825 crores, nearly INR10,000 crores, with a growth of 21% year-on-year.

subsections.label: Export Demand
points: Exports business unit crossed the 6,00,000 units mark for the second consecutive quarter, clocking 25% growth year-on-year.
Latin America continues to outperform, delivering sustained growth for 11 consecutive quarters.
Asia recorded double-digit growth, particularly due to Sri Lanka, Philippines and Nepal.

subsections.label: Domestic Demand
points: Domestic motorcycles saw a growth of around 11% for the full year.
The 125cc plus segment grew faster than the 100cc segment.
The 150cc plus segment was the fastest growing.

subsections.label: Motorcycles
points: Pulsar brand delivered record export volume for the quarter.
KTM and Triumph brands delivered a record domestic performance in quarter 4 with combined volumes of nearly 43,000 units.
The N and NS series of Pulsar grew at over twice the rate of the industry in Q4.

subsections.label: Electric Vehicles
points: Chetak crossed the 1 lakh retail mark for the very first time in a single quarter.
Chetak's market share moved up to almost 23%, a gain of 170 basis points sequentially.
In FY26, Chetak crossed the 5-lakh unit mark at INR4,000 crores in revenue.

subsections.label: Commercial Vehicles
points: The commercial vehicle business crossed the 5-lakh unit mark for the full year.
The ICE franchise remains rock solid with a share in the CNG segment close to 90%.

subsections.label: Regional Performance
points: Latin America remains a key driver of export performance in terms of volume, revenue and EBITDA.
Brazil sales nearly 10,000 units during the quarter, supported by expanded manufacturing capacity.
Nigeria reached some stability in Q4 with volumes crossing 1 lakh units.

subsections.label: Logistics and Supply Chain
points: Supply chain difficulties in terms of the LPG shortage, manpower availability and outbound logistics to overseas markets have impaired availability to service demand by about 10% to 15%.
Despite disruptions, logistics in export have not lost any sale.

subsections.label: Commodity Inflation
points: The cost environment is seeing a potential rise during the quarter of about 3% to 5%, driven by the metal complex.
Significant inflation is now visible across almost the entire basket with not just noble metals, but also base metals like steel and aluminium.

subsections.label: Geopolitical Risks
points: There is a potential for further disruptions due to the geopolitical issues in the Middle East.

subsections.label: Outlook
points: We expect our growth tempo to actually continue in Q1 basis the strong competitive positions we enjoy in the key segments.
We are looking at moving the exports needle to 2,20,000 units per month this quarter, up from the 2,00,000 levels.
The 150cc plus segment and electric segments, which are the absolute epicentre of our focus, mitigate the overall softening of the demand.

guidance_ledger.statement: We are looking at moving the exports needle to 2,20,000 units per month this quarter, up from the 2,00,000 levels.
The motorcycle category from its rocking 20% growth in Q4 to we estimate 7% to 9% in the near term.
The 150cc plus segment should grow at twice the industry rate.
The electric category in both 3-wheelers and 2-wheelers will witness not just continuity of growth, but perhaps a further increase in growth.
We expect to outpace industry growth in the 125cc plus segment and particularly in the 150cc plus segment and continue on the path of market share acquisition.
We expect to start to scale up Riki beginning quarter 1.
The industry should improve its growth rate by 6 to 8 percentage points.
We are taking steps to build three-wheeler capacity, particularly in the e-vehicle segment.
We will aim to maintain the leadership position here on.

Quarter 4 was a standout quarter with revenues crossing INR16,000 crores, EBITDA at INR3,300 crores and margins at 20.8%.
Exports business unit crossed the 6,00,000 units mark for the second consecutive quarter, clocking 25% growth year-on-year.
Latin America continues to outperform, delivering sustained growth for 11 consecutive quarters now and reporting another all-time high performance.
Domestic motorcycles saw a growth of around 11% for the full year.
Chetak crossed the 1 lakh retail mark for the very first time in a single quarter, the highest ever quarterly retails for the brand.
The commercial vehicle business crossed the 5-lakh unit mark for the full year, a milestone that reflects both our ICE leadership and the scaling up of our electric portfolio.
The cost environment is seeing a potential rise during the quarter of about 3% to 5%, driven by the metal complex.

quarters.summary: Bajaj Auto Ltd expects continued growth momentum in exports and domestic markets, driven by product refreshes and strategic initiatives, despite commodity cost pressures.

subsections.label: Financial Highlights
points: The company delivered its best-ever quarterly performance across revenues and profitability, underpinned by record volumes.
Domestic revenues reached an all-time high during the quarter, driven by double-digit revenue growth across all businesses.
Export revenues grew double digit in 8 out of the last 9 quarters.
EBITDA margins expanded to 20.8%, up 30 basis points sequentially.
Profit after tax after a onetime adjustment stood at INR2,503 crores, rising 19% year-on-year.

subsections.label: Demand Drivers
points: The momentum was set by the GST rate cuts, which sustained itself beyond the festive period.
Domestic motorcycles saw growth post-GST rationalization, with the industry growing by about 15% in Q3.
The CV segment in exports grew at 56% this quarter.

subsections.label: Motorcycles
points: Domestic motorcycles saw growth post-GST rationalization, with the industry growing by about 15% in Q3.
The 125cc plus segment is growing at a faster rate, with the 150cc plus segment growing even faster.
Pulsar received the benefit of consumer behavior transitioning from cautious to optimistic.

subsections.label: Commercial Vehicles
points: The 3-wheeler category was positively impacted by the rationalization of GST.
The Electric segment continues to drive the 3-wheeler industry by growing at about 50% plus.
Bajaj Auto maintains a 70% plus market share in the ICE segment.

subsections.label: Pro-Biking
points: The Pro-Biking business delivered a record domestic performance with combined volumes of over 35,000 units.
KTM volumes were led by strong demand across the Adventure portfolio.
Triumph maintained a healthy momentum despite the GST rationalization.

key_figures.label: EV portfolio contribution to domestic revenues

subsections.label: Electric Vehicles
points: The EV portfolio now contributes to 25% of domestic revenues.
The EV business now delivers double-digit EBITDA margin while improving unit economics.
Chetak regained nearly 500 basis points of market share during the quarter.

subsections.label: Exports
points: The BU crossed the 200,000 average per month sales level in October '25 after nearly 40 months.
Nigeria, the largest market, doubled sales in Q3 compared to Q2.
Latam delivered its highest ever quarterly performance powered by Colombia.

subsections.label: KTM AG Turnaround
points: Bajaj ownership in KTM Austria increased to 75% and a turnaround plan was commenced.
The focus will be on operational turnaround and putting the business back on track.
The Supervisory and Management Boards have been reconstituted to align with the revised ownership structure.

subsections.label: Retail Finance
points: BACL delivered a class-leading performance with penetration scaling to 45% levels.
BACL continues to enjoy the highest level of creditworthiness with AAA ratings.

guidance_ledger.statement: Overall, the Exports business has established a sustained growth momentum, and we expect this growth tempo to continue in the coming months.
We expect this growth momentum in the industry to continue and the motorcycle industry to continue to grow at, say, 12% to 15%.
We expect to outpace industry growth in the 125cc plus segment and particularly in the 150cc plus segment and continue on the path of market share acquisition.
We expect to start to scale up Riki beginning quarter 1.
The industry should improve its growth rate by 6 to 8 percentage points.
We are taking steps to build three-wheeler capacity, particularly in the e-vehicle segment.
We will aim to maintain the leadership position here on.

pressing_questions: What is the updated timeline for the new 125cc bike that Bajaj Auto was looking to introduce this year?
How should we think about the salience of commodities like precious metals, copper, and aluminium given the continuous inflation?
What is the growth outlook for exports for the remaining few months of this year and next year, and which will be the key markets driving that growth?

quarters.summary: Bajaj Auto Ltd anticipates continued growth in exports and domestic markets, driven by strategic initiatives and product launches, despite challenges in supply chain and market dynamics.

subsections.label: Currency and Commodities
points: Quarter 2 saw a weakening of the rupee, benefiting export realization and margins.
Dollar realization improved to 87.1 compared to 85.6 in the previous quarter.
Net cost inflation impact was about 40 basis points, driven by increases in steel, noble metals, copper, and rubber.

subsections.label: Financial Results
points: Quarterly revenue from operations was nearly Rs. 15,000 crores, a 14% growth from the previous highest.
Exports revenue reached a new pinnacle, driven by strong volume growth and better currency realization.
EBITDA crossed Rs. 3,000 crores for the first time, with margins at 20.5%.
PAT was a little under Rs. 2,500 crores, up 24% year-on-year.

subsections.label: Cash and Investments
points: Generated around Rs. 4,500 crores of free cash in the first half of the year.
Surplus cash is in excess of Rs. 14,000 crores at the end of the quarter.
Paid a dividend of nearly Rs. 6,000 crores and made strategic investments over Rs. 2,000 crores.

subsections.label: Export Performance
points: The BU grew volumes by 24% in Q2 with exports of over 550,000 units.
Top 30 overseas markets grew about 14% in this quarter.
Sales in Bajaj Brazil clocked 8,000 units in Q2, with October retails crossing 3,000 units.
The CV segment in exports delivered a growth of 67% this quarter.

subsections.label: Domestic Motorcycles
points: The domestic motorcycle industry experienced a rollercoaster performance with a small decline of 1% in April-August.
The Pulsar portfolio was at an all-time peak performance during the festive period.
Market share gains began in the strategic 125cc plus segment, driven by the 150cc plus sport segment.

subsections.label: Commercial Vehicles
points: The three-wheeler BU delivered an all-time record quarter of 145,000 units.
In ICE, high levels of 75% market share were maintained.
In the EV segment, there was a 15% shortfall from plan due to supply chain constraints.

subsections.label: Electric Two-Wheelers
points: Chetak regained the number 1 position in terms of Vahan Registration in October.
The launch of a new model by early next year is expected to strengthen leadership.
The EV portfolio delivered almost 20% of domestic revenue with a double-digit EBITDA percentage in Q2.

subsections.label: Pro-Biking
points: KTM and Triumph brands sold over 30,000 units, registering a 30% growth.
The launch of the Duke 160 targets the 150cc super premium segment.
GST rate cuts impacted some KTM and all Triumph models, but prices were maintained.

subsections.label: Regional Performance
points: LATAM delivered its highest ever quarterly performance, powered by Colombia and Mexico.
Sales in Bajaj Brazil clocked 8,000 units in Q2, with October retails crossing 3,000 units.
Both Asia and Africa delivered double-digit growth with standout performances in Sri Lanka, Philippines, and East Africa.

subsections.label: Supply Chain Constraints
points: The EV segment faced supply chain constraints, particularly in e-components and manufacturing capacity.
Recovery in the supply chain is ahead of time, with a shift to more secure LRE-based components.

subsections.label: Market Strategy
points: An engaging thematic campaign for Pulsar brand and introduction of new variants contributed to record performance.
The spare business delivered an all-time high performance of Rs. 1,800 crores, growing at 21%.

subsections.label: BACL Performance
points: BACL added over 2 lakh customers in Q2 with AUM crossing Rs. 14,000 crores.
Q2 PAT was above plan, coming in at over Rs. 132 crores.

subsections.label: KTM Acquisition
points: Bajaj Auto is in the process of acquiring full control of KTM, with regulatory approvals nearing completion.
An extraordinary general meeting is scheduled to change the company name to Bajaj Mobility AG.

guidance_ledger.statement: The industry should improve its growth rate by 6 to 8 percentage points.
We expect exports to continue to maintain the growth tempo.
We will aim to outpace the industry growth in the 125cc plus segment.
We are taking steps to build three-wheeler capacity, particularly in the e-vehicle segment.
We intend to scale E-Rick in a few months from now.
We will aim to maintain the leadership position here on.

risk_register.note: Constraints in e-components and manufacturing capacity impacted EV segment.
Cost pressures across key commodities, including steel and noble metals.

themes.note: Supply chain constraints in the EV segment are easing, with recovery ahead of schedule.

key_takeaways: The Q2 performance has built on the strong note on which we commenced Q1 FY '26 and it has recorded several all-time highs.
The BU grew volumes by 24% in Q2 with exports of over 550,000 units.
LATAM delivered its highest ever quarterly performance, powered by Colombia and Mexico.
The CV segment in exports delivered a growth of 67% this quarter.
The domestic motorcycle industry experienced a rollercoaster performance with a small decline of 1% in April-August.
The three-wheeler BU delivered an all-time record quarter of 145,000 units.
Chetak regained the number 1 position in terms of Vahan Registration in October.
KTM and Triumph brands sold over 30,000 units, registering a 30% growth.
pressing_questions: If you can sort of share a little bit more perspective on what you have seen post-GST in terms of customer behavior in terms of while you called out some premiumization, but it was good to know what are the behavioral changes you are seeing pre- and post-GST?
Also, can you share the festive growth number in itself? How did it fare for you if you had that number handy?
And a clarification of the 6%-8% growth for the remainder of the year, is this for the entire industry or is it for motorcycles?
